pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this file at this exact path for user 'ga'
Task description and export_result.sh reference this path
Task description and export_result.sh reference this path

A complete FTA based on spec requires 3 OR gates (top level, Battery A failure modes, Battery B failure modes), but metadata specifies minimum 2. This is consistent with verifier.py line 187 (checks >= 2). The value 2 represents the minimum to demonstrate understanding of failure redundancy.

Specification states: 'The Battery System only fails if BOTH Battery A fails AND Battery B fails simultaneously.' This requires exactly 1 AND gate to represent the redundancy logic. Verified against setup_task.sh specification section 4.

Specification section 5 defines two battery failure modes: 'Internal Cell Malfunction' and 'Connector Disconnect'. With two batteries, this yields 4 basic events: Cell A, Connector A, Cell B, Connector B. Note: Verifier.py checks for >= 3 (line 197), which is less strict than the metadata claim of 4.

pi_items.metadata_value: Real (6S LiPo batteries, XT90 connectors, Motor ESCs, PDB with active OR-ing circuit)
pi_items.verified_value: Real components used in actual drone systems
pi_items.source: Web search verification: 6S LiPo batteries (22.2V nominal) are standard in commercial drones; XT90 connectors are standard high-current connectors with ~90A rating; Motor ESCs are Electronic Speed Controllers standard in drones; Active diode OR-ing circuits are real power distribution technology documented by TI, Analog Devices, etc.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task (fictional Drone-X1) but uses real drone components and technology. The fault tree requires 1 AND gate to represent battery redundancy (both must fail), minimum 2 OR gates (though a complete tree needs 3), and minimum 4 basic events representing the two failure modes per battery. The system models a realistic power distribution scenario: the PDB is a single point of failure, but redundant batteries are protected by an active OR-ing circuit requiring both to fail before power is lost. All file paths and metadata values are consistent with the task specification.
